text_draft:
Susie's mom opened the back door.
The toys tumbled out into the snow—the wooden train, the doll, the crayons—Pink Bear leading the way.
"What are you doing?" Susie's mom laughed.
"Clearing a path," Susie called. "For the hunt!"
And it worked. The toys, dragged through the snow, carved little trails. Not perfect. But enough.
Pink Bear looked back at the big bright window where Susie watched, her blonde bob pressed against the glass.
The garden was becoming possible again.
